body.text: God is He who raised the heavens without pillars that ye see;
the foundations of the House of God,

transported, the seed-produce from the place in which he had reaped it,
to the place in which the grain was to be trodden out.

(Lh, S, K.) [This last signification is said in the TA to be tropical; but according to a passage of the Msb quoted in the first sentence of this art., it is proper. In most of the phrases here following, the verb is undoubtedly used tropically.] ―         -b2-

how it is exalted in respect of excellence, and exaltation of rank.

abaseth him, so that He maketh the unjust to overcome him,
in order to try his creatures, in the present world. (Az, TA.) [See also art.

body.text: He does not put away the staff,

] is an allusion to discipline, chastisement, or punishment, (Mgh, TA,) or to severity thereof, (Msb,) and to beating (Mgh, TA) of women; (Mgh;) not meaning that the staff, or stick, is on the shoulder: (Msb:) or the first is an allusion to many journeyings. (TA.) ―         -b20-
; (Mgh, Msb;) so in the “ Firdows, ” on the authority of 'Alee and I' Ab and 'Áïsheh, meaning

persons;] a saying of Mohammad, which means that nothing is recorded either for or against three persons; (Mgh, Msb; *) these three being the sleeper until he awakes, the afflicted with disease or the like, or the demented, until he recovers, and the child until he becomes big, or attains to puberty. (El-Jámi' -es-Sagheer of Es-Suyootee; in which we find
.) This is like the saying next before mentioned; the pen having never been put [to the tablet to record aught] against the child. (Msb.) ―         -b21-  [
